title: fix: flatten nova-fast to single-target Bedrock config
author: voodoohop

gist.headline: Untangling nova-fast
gist.blurb: The nova-fast model got a little too clever with its routing and started tripping over itself. We simplified the wiring, and now it actually generates text instead of errors.
gist.summary: Simplified the nova-fast model configuration to use a direct Bedrock target, resolving a bug that caused widespread 500 errors.
gist.impact: The nova-fast model is fully operational again and will reliably return text generations.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee fixing a complex, tangled mess of wires by replacing it with a single, straight connection. The Nomnom creature is eating the old, overly-complex routing configuration.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of a cute pixel art bee mascot with a yellow body and dark brown horizontal stripes, small black legs, two black antennae, black vertical oval eyes, bright pink rectangular blush marks on cheeks, a small happy smile, and small rounded light blue translucent wings. She is wearing a tiny tool belt and untangling a messy knot of wires into a single clean connection. The round Nomnom creature happily eats the discarded tangled wires. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and pastel lavender gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
